# Awwwards Output Spec

## Mobile spec (its own design, not a stack)

- Hero: <FILL IN — what changes from desktop?>
- Navigation: <FILL IN>
- Signature interaction equivalent: <FILL IN>
- Typography scale changes: <FILL IN>
- Media weight: <FILL IN — total KB target>

## Performance spec

Tighter than the generic budget if award candidacy is real:

| Metric | Target |
|---|---|
| LCP (mobile mid-tier, 4G) | < 2.0s |
| CLS | < 0.05 |
| INP | < 150ms |
| Total JS gzipped | < 200 KB |
| Total image weight, hero pages | < 700 KB |

## Accessibility spec

WCAG 2.2 AA is the floor. For award candidacy, also:

- [ ] Keyboard-only complete-task verified on desktop and mobile.
- [ ] Reduced-motion path is its own designed experience.
- [ ] All non-decorative animation has an alt-text or live-region for screen readers when state changes.
- [ ] Color contrast passes for the brand's actual brand colors, not adjusted brand colors.

## What award-level adds beyond ship-level

| Dimension | Ship-level threshold | Award-level threshold |
|---|---|---|
| Taste rubric average | ≥ 7.5 | ≥ 8.5 |
| Lowest rubric category | ≥ 6.5 | ≥ 7.5 |
| Brave decision count | ≥ 0 | ≥ 1 named |
| Mobile is its own design | recommended | required |
| Signature interaction concept-tied | recommended | required |
| Memorable single screenshot | nice to have | required |

If the project is below award-level, document it here honestly. A great ship-level site is a better deliverable than a mediocre award attempt.
